mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expansion in aggregate demand, which boosts corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is negative in more than 40% of quarters with positive GDP growth, or rank IC over a 3-year rolling window is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, it signals severe labor market slack that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If triggered instances show positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and declines in housing starts represent a supply-led expansion where investment outpaces residential construction, a historically positive configuration for equity returns as capacity utilization rises.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (INDPRO increasing and HOUST decreasing) yields negative average 20-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term in a regression is not significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 1%), the Federal Reserve has limited room to ease further, so any positive economic data raises expectations of rate normalization, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If in periods when FEDFUNDS < 1%, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the rank IC over such periods is below -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp one-month drop in nonfarm payrolls (more than 100,000 jobs lost) triggers immediate fears of recession, causing rapid selling in equities over the following trading week as risk appetite collapses.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 100,000 in a single month
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the average return is not significantly negative at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand that drives corporate revenue growth, leading to increasing S&P 500 valuations over a one-month horizon as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 45% of months with positive RSAFS growth, or rank IC over a 3-year window is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When both consumer price inflation and the federal funds rate are rising simultaneously, real interest rates become less negative, increasing the discount rate on equity cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint positive movement in both series does not lead to negative average 20-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term in a predictive regression is not significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: In the early stage of a housing recovery, rising housing starts (HOUST) signal increased residential investment that boosts economic activity and consumer wealth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as the recovery broadens.
regime_hint: post-recession housing recovery (HOUST rising from trough)
failure_condition: If during identified housing recovery phases the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of periods, or the average return is not significantly positive at 5% level.
